plugins.name: video-bug-analyzer
plugins.description: Diagnose UI and rendering bugs from a screen recording — and reason over any clip's look + motion. Give Claude a video and roughly when the bug happens; the plugin extracts frames (contact sheet, scene-cut detection, per-timestamp zoom + before/after strips, ROI time-stack --stack) and reasons over them to pinpoint the defect. Quantifies FPS stutter / dropped frames + freeze gaps (--stutter) and frame-pacing jitter (--pacing). Doubles as an art/colour-reference tool: --palette pulls a clip's hex ramp, --palette --over-time emits the colour arc (t,[hex...] per window), and --loop-check reports the first-vs-last seam diff for a seamless loop; GIF input works on every mode. Runs never overwrite a previous extraction; --check-update spots a stale install. Requires ffmpeg (auto-installs where the sandbox allows).

plugins.name: repo-bootstrap
plugins.description: Set up a repo to use portka-tools plugins (writes .claude/settings.json + optional CI; --print-only for a classifier-safe manual mode), and optionally install the Portka standard setup: a workflow CLAUDE.md (branch-per-change, releases left to a human, tool feedback funneled to the marketplace's issues not stray branches), a git/gh permissions allowlist, and an enforced SemVer version sync bound to the repo's existing version (package.json / pyproject.toml / Cargo.toml / VERSION / README) with a basic test suite whose CHANGELOG check anchors to a real release heading — plus a native version-sync test (node:test / unittest) and a wired-up npm test for JS/Python repos.

plugins.name: app-website-evaluator
plugins.description: Evaluate an app or website with a standardized, coverage-honest scorecard — each dimension 0-100 + letter grade, a weight-averaged overall that is starred when unassessed weight is excluded, and optional --json. Scores a live --url, a local --dir build, or pre-fetched --html (file or stdin, +--headers) so an agent behind a sandbox egress proxy still gets the full scorecard. Security credits source-visible controls (a <meta> CSP, security.txt, no third-party <script> origins). AI-readiness parse-validates JSON-LD and credits rich schema types (FAQPage/Review/Article). Covers SEO, crawlability, AI-readiness, social/sharing assets, security, performance, and growth — tailored to the site's type and community.

plugins.name: tab-chord-formatter
plugins.description: Format a messy guitar tab or chord sheet into a clean, readable layout for screen, or render a consistent monospace PDF songbook (one or many songs, a target songs-per-page) via headless Chromium. Standardizes [Section] labels, aligns chords over the right lyric syllables, tidies a metadata header (Title/Artist/Capo/Key/Tuning) and 6-line ASCII tab blocks — a deterministic script does the cleanup and rendering, the skill applies judgment to re-align and infer structure.
